name: Palmetto First
description_short: Palmetto First Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union offering checking, savings, auto loans, personal loans, and financial services to local members.
description_long:
Palmetto First Federal Credit Union operates as a federally-chartered credit union serving members in their region. Like all credit unions, it is member-owned and not-for-profit, regulated by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) rather than the FDIC. The organization emphasizes personalized local lending with named loan officers (Viola and Jessica) available to guide members through the application process. Palmetto First positions itself as a community-focused alternative to traditional banks.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products. Members can open free checking accounts, establish savings accounts with flexible savings plans, apply for personal loans for debt consolidation and unexpected expenses, and finance auto purchases through competitive auto loans. The website highlights specific savings comparisons—claiming members save approximately $171 per year in interest on a $25,000 auto loan financed over 60 months compared to banks, or roughly $855 over five years. They provide loan applications and payment processing through digital channels and maintain access to the CO-OP network for ATM services.

Palmetto First distinguishes itself through emphasis on local relationships and straightforward messaging around cost savings. The marketing prominently features named loan officers to create personal touchpoints, and the brand positions credit union membership as inherently more economical than traditional banking. They highlight that families in credit unions save approximately $159 annually compared to bank customers. The tone is casual and member-focused rather than corporate.

As a regional credit union, Palmetto First's primary limitation is membership eligibility—not all consumers can join without meeting specific criteria (typically geographic, employment, or family relationships). The website content provided does not detail membership requirements, underwriting standards, credit score minimums, or full product details like APR ranges. No information is available about financial education resources, mobile app features, or whether they offer business services. The company's digital presence appears limited to basic product descriptions without comprehensive disclosure documents.
services: Savings accounts with flexible savings plans
Auto loans (new and used vehicle financing)
Personal loans (consolidation, unexpected expenses, high-interest debt payoff)
Member account login and online banking portal
pros: Free checking accounts with no monthly fees
Member-owned structure means profits return to members rather than shareholders
Demonstrated savings on auto loans: average $171/year vs. banks ($855 over 5 years)
Personalized local lending with named loan officers available
Access to CO-OP ATM network for nationwide cash access
Personal loans available for consolidation and debt management
Digital loan application and payment processing available
cons: Membership eligibility restricted—not open to general public
No APR ranges, rate details, or underwriting criteria disclosed on website
Limited digital information about loan terms, fees, or product details
No evidence of credit-building products, credit monitoring, or financial education resources
Regional credit union may have limited branch/ATM availability compared to national banks
best_for: Members eligible for credit union membership seeking lower-cost checking and savings
Auto borrowers looking to minimize interest costs on vehicle financing
Consumers needing personal loans for debt consolidation or emergency expenses
Individuals prioritizing member-owned institutions over for-profit banks
diagnosis: Palmetto First Federal Credit Union is best for consumers already eligible for membership who want to reduce banking costs through a member-owned credit union structure, particularly those financing vehicles or consolidating debt. The main caveat is that membership is restricted to eligible individuals, and the website lacks specific rate, term, and underwriting details necessary for prospective members to evaluate actual loan costs.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Palmetto First — Credit Union in Florence, SC | CreditDoc
meta_description: Palmetto First — NCUA-insured credit union in Florence, SC. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
